title: viburnum furcatum var. melanophyllum
intro: Viburnum furcatum var. melanophyllum, also known as the black-veined viburnum or black-veined wayfaring tree, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its dark green leaves and clusters of small white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and antiviral properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Viburnum furcatum var. melanophyllum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be found in some commercial supplements and health products.
